etymology.description: 禾(禾) (hé, "plant") + 又(又) (yòu, "hand") = a hand holding a plant by the stalk -> handle
etymology.interpretation: The depiction of a hand 又(又) grasping a plant 禾(禾) conveys the fundamental act of holding, emphasizing control or management over the object, which is extended metaphorically to mean 'handle'.

examples.chinese: 他秉持正直的原则(他秉持正直的原則)。
管理者应当秉公处理事情(管理者應當秉公處理事情)。
examples.pinyin: Guǎnlǐ zhě yīngdāng bǐnggōng chǔlǐ shìqíng.
